back_translation: He got into a boat, crossed to the other side, and came to His own town.
Behold, some people carried a paralyzed man who was lying on a mat to Him. When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralyzed man, "My child, take courage; your sins have been forgiven."
Behold, some of the scribes were thinking in their hearts, "This man is blaspheming God."
Jesus knew their thoughts and said, "Why do you think evil in your hearts?
Which is easier — to say, 'Your sins have been forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'?
But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins..." Then He said to the paralyzed man, "Get up, take up your mat, and go home."
And he got up and went home.
When the crowds saw this event, they were filled with reverential awe and glorified God, who had given such authority to humans.
As Jesus went on from there, He saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax booth, and He said to him, "Follow Me." Matthew then got up and followed Him.
While He was reclining at table in the house, behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and ate together with Jesus and His disciples.
When the Pharisees saw this, they asked His disciples, "Why does your Teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?"
When Jesus heard this, He said, "Healthy people do not need a doctor, but sick people do.
But go and study to understand this: 'I desire mercy, not sacrifice.' For I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners."
Then the disciples of John came to Him and asked, "Why is it that we and the Pharisees fast often, but Your disciples do not fast?"
Jesus replied to them, "Can the bridegroom's friends mourn while the bridegroom is still with them? But a day will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and on that day they will fast.
No one takes unshrunk new cloth to patch an old garment, because the patching cloth will tear the garment, and the tear will become even worse.

Nor does anyone pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the wineskins will burst, the wine will spill, and the wineskins will be ruined along with it. But new wine must be poured into new wineskins, and then both will be preserved."

While He was still saying these things to them, behold, a certain leader came, bowed down before Him, and said, "My daughter has just died this very moment. But please come and lay Your hand on her, and she will live."
Jesus got up and followed him, together with His disciples.
Behold, a certain woman who had suffered from a discharge-of-blood illness for twelve years approached from behind and touched the edge of His garment.
For she was thinking in her heart, "If only I can touch His garment, I will be healed."
Jesus turned and saw her, and said, "Daughter, take courage; your faith has made you well." And the woman was healed at that very moment.
When Jesus entered the leader's house and saw the flute players and the crowd making a loud noise,
He said, "Go away; the girl is not dead, but she is only sleeping." They laughed at Him scornfully.
When the crowd had been driven out, He went in and took her hand, and the girl got up.
And this report spread throughout that region.
As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed Him, crying out, "Son of David, have mercy on us!"
When He went into the house, the two blind men came to Him. Jesus said to them, "Do you believe that I am able to do this?" They said to Him, "We believe, Lord."
Then He touched their eyes and said, "Let it be done according to your faith."
Their eyes were opened. Jesus sternly charged them, saying, "Be careful not to let anyone know about this."
But they went out and proclaimed the story of Him throughout that region.
As they were leaving, behold, some people brought a mute man who was demon-possessed to Him.
When the demon had been driven out, the mute man was able to speak. The crowds marveled and said, "Nothing like this has ever appeared in Israel."
But the Pharisees said, "He drives out demons by the ruler of demons."
Jesus went throughout all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness.
When He saw the crowds, He had compassion on them, because they were weary and scattered like sheep without a shepherd.
Then He said to His disciples, "The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few.
Therefore ask the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into His harvest."
